content.problemStatement:
You are given a string SSS. Find the number of ways to choose an unordered pair of non-overlapping non-empty substrings of this string (let's denote them by s1s_1s1​ and s2s_2s2​ in such a way that s2s_2s2​ starts after s1s_1s1​ ends) such that their concatenation s1+s2s_1 + s_2s1​+s2​ is a palindrome.

Two pairs (s1,s2)(s_1, s_2)(s1​,s2​) and (s1′,s2′)(s_1', s_2')(s1′​,s2′​) are different if s1s_1s1​ is chosen at a different position from s1′s_1's1′​ or s2s_2s2​ is chosen at a different position from s2′s_2's2′​.
constraints: SSS contains only lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: The first and only line of the input contains a single string SSS.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer — the number of ways to choose a valid pair of substrings.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The following pairs of substrings can be chosen: ("a", "a"), ("a", "ba"), ("a", "bba"), ("ab", "a"), ("ab", "ba"), ("abb", "a"), ("b", "b").
